etymology.description: semantic 尸(尸) (shī, "corpse") + phonetic 古(古) (gǔ, "ancient") -> reside; live; dwell
etymology.interpretation: The character 居(jū, "reside; live; dwell") combines 尸(尸) (shī, "corpse"), suggesting a place one occupies, with 古(古) (gǔ, "ancient"), which provides the phonetic element. This reflects the idea of dwelling or living in a specific place.

examples.chinese: 他居住在北京(他居住在北京)。
他们居家办公(他們居家辦公)，减少了通勤时间(減少了通勤時間)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen jūjiā bàngōng, jiǎnshǎo le tōngqín shíjiān.
examples.english: They work from home, reducing commuting time.

examples.chinese: 古代诗人常隐居在山林(古代詩人常隱居在山林)，以避世俗(以避世俗)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài shīrén cháng yǐnjū zài shānlín, yǐ bì shìsú.
examples.english: Ancient poets often secluded themselves in the mountains to escape worldly affairs.
